# Electron build root: boot the Agent OS + harness windows in one shell

## Context

The head of the PoC critical path for Epic #13012 (per the recorded gut-estimate: the ONE item with genuine discovery risk; everything else parallelizes in dev-server mode). Shell decision already made at the parent (operator, 2026-06-12): **Electron — always Chromium + always Node.js**; Tauri retired. This leaf creates the build root.

## The Problem

The harness needs a packaged shell that boots the whole organism: the Electron main process hosting (or supervising) the Agent OS — orchestrator + MCP servers — while windows serve the harness Neo app on one SharedWorker heap. No such packaging root exists; the repo ships dev-server + daemon topologies only.

## The Architectural Reality

- **Topology decision carried from planning (the risk item):** target = **Agent OS in-process in the Electron main** (Brain + Body, one install). Untested: MCP server lifecycle, port management, and restart semantics inside Electron main (restart affordances incl. settle-or-reject promise semantics are a #13015 lifecycle requirement). **Sanctioned fallback (caps the risk):** the main process *supervises the Agent OS as a child process* (today's daemon topology) — the PoC criterion survives either way; in-process becomes an MVP refinement if the spike fights back.
- **Source-tree discipline (parent decision):** the build root wraps built Body + Brain WITHOUT mixing source trees — own `package.json`, main-process entry, window management; placement via `structural-pre-flight` at implementation (a packaging root is the sanctioned exception shape discussed at the parent: source stays in `apps/` + `ai/`).
- **Multi-window:** Chromium popups on one SharedWorker heap (the shipping shared-apps pattern); the shell adds `BrowserWindow` management + the Electron enhancement points consumed later by #13025/#13028/#13030 (exact spawn-at-position, move events, OS chrome control).
- **Web mode stays the development convenience + goodie** — the shell never becomes a dev-loop dependency.

## Contract Ledger (new surfaces — this leaf INTRODUCES them)

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Electron build root (`package.json`, main entry) | this leaf (new) | boots Agent OS (in-process target / child-process fallback) + first harness window | dev-server mode untouched | README in the packaging root | parent topology decision |
| Main-process ↔ Agent OS lifecycle | this leaf (new) | start/stop/restart hooks consumable by the #13015 lifecycle service; restarts settle-or-reject pending promises | child-process supervision = the fallback arm | JSDoc + root README | #13015 guardrail |
| Window-management bridge | this leaf (new) | BrowserWindow creation/positioning for popups on the shared heap | `window.open` path keeps working (web mode) | JSDoc | shipping shared-apps pattern |

Rows altitude-honest; shapes reconcile in the implementing PR.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A packaged Electron app boots: main process brings up the Agent OS (in-process OR documented child-process fallback — the spike outcome is recorded either way), one window serves the harness app.
- [ ] Chromium popup windows join the same SharedWorker heap (multi-window proof inside the shell).
- [ ] The topology spike outcome (in-process vs fallback, with the friction found) is documented in the PR + reconciled into this Ledger.
- [ ] Dev-server/browser mode remains fully functional (no shell dependency leaks into the dev loop).

## Out of Scope

- Settings pane content (#13015 leaves), themes (#13022), store/auto-update distribution (follow-up leaf once the root exists).
- The agentos-PoC replacement app itself (own leaf; this root LOADS whatever app exists).

## Related

- Parent: Epic #13012. Milestone: M1 (the shell is the login vehicle). Board: Project 13.
- Consumers: #13015 (lifecycle), #13025/#13028/#13030 (Electron enhancement points), #13022 (themes render inside it).
- Closed Electron priors swept: #12958 (worker reply-loss, Electron-deterministic — its fix ships in v13), #12419 (macOS-Electron parent-chain-walk) — both inform, neither overlaps.

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ **Intake + build plan + topology-spike methodology** (Ada / @neo-opus-ada). Intake verdict: **valid-as-written**; epic-review prerequisite satisfied by citation (the #13019 two-review cap supersedes per-identity review; #13012 quota FILLED — Vega `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABF9uZVA` 🟢 + GPT `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABF9w2Jg` 🟢). Authored in a headless session; the boot-dependent ACs run in an Electron-capable environment — this comment is the methodology that session executes against.
> 
> ### Structural pre-flight — placement DECIDED
> 
> New **top-level packaging root** with its own `package.json`. Considered + rejected: repo root (ADR 0020 §3 forbids touching the single source `package.json`), `apps/` (browser-app *source* built via the root webpack; the ADR keeps harness UI source there but the packaging root is a separate **built-output wrapper**). Binding authority: ADR 0020 §3 ("own `package.json`, wraps built Body + Brain, no source-tree mixing"). Exact dir name is a file-authoring sub-decision under structural-pre-flight; the *class* is fixed. **Map-maintenance is a Blocking AC** — a new top-level packaging role needs a `learn/benefits/ArchitectureOverview.md` Structural Inventory row in the implementing PR.
> 
> ### The core discovery — where does the Agent OS run?
> 
> Two arms; the spike runs A first and falls to B on the first hard blocker, recording which.
> 
> **Arm A (target) — Agent OS in-process in the Electron main.** Electron main IS Node, so it can import `ai/daemons/orchestrator` + the `ai/mcp/server/*` servers directly. **Four falsifiers (any one → fall back to B, record the specific one):**
> 1. **Native-module ABI** *(highest-probability forcing function)* — Chroma's client and the graph's SQLite binding are native addons compiled for system Node; Electron ships its own Node ABI. Standard fix is `electron-rebuild`, but if the rebuild fights the Chroma/embedder stack, that's a fallback signal, not a multi-day yak-shave.
> 2. **Crash isolation** — an MCP-server/daemon crash must NOT take down the Electron main (a fleet cockpit has to survive a server crash). No process boundary in Arm A → if a server fault propagates to the UI, fallback.
> 3. **Port management** — the three MCP servers + orchestrator bind ports; verify clean allocation/teardown within main's lifecycle (no collision, no leak across restarts).
> 4. **Restart semantics** — #13015 requires settle-or-reject promise semantics on runtime MCP-server restart. If an in-process server can't tear down + re-init while cleanly settling pending promises, fallback.
> 
> **Arm B (sanctioned fallback) — main supervises the Agent OS as child processes.** This is *today's daemon topology*, re-parented under Electron main (`child_process.fork`/`spawn`). Wins process isolation (falsifier 2 gone), runs native modules in system-Node children (falsifier 1 gone), and restart = kill+respawn with clean settle-or-reject (falsifier 4 trivial). Cost: IPC overhead + loses the "one in-process install" elegance — but it satisfies the **PoC criterion identically** ("@tobiu starts one agent from the UI").
> 
> ### Multi-window proof (independent of the topology arm — de-risk separately)
> 
> The shipping reference is `apps/colors` (`Neo.manager.Window` God-View + `getWindowAt(x,y)` + `main.addon.WindowPosition`, the #8164→#9498 lineage). **Electron-specific risk to verify for AC2:** SharedWorker sharing across `BrowserWindow`s needs same-origin scope. `file://` origins can break SharedWorker sharing; if so, serve the built app over an in-main loopback HTTP server (a standard Electron pattern) so both windows share the heap. This is the AC2 unknown worth isolating first.
> 
> ### Verifiable PR slices (build order for the runnable env)
> 
> 1. **Shell skeleton** — packaging root `package.json` + main entry opens ONE `BrowserWindow` loading a built Neo app over loopback. *AC: shell boots, one window serves the app.* (No Agent OS yet — isolates shell risk.)
> 2. **Multi-window** — popup `BrowserWindow` joins the same SharedWorker heap (loopback-origin if `file://` fails). *AC2.*
> 3. **Topology spike** — Arm A attempt with the four falsifiers as the decision gate; record the outcome + any forced fallback to Arm B with the specific blocker. *AC1 + AC3, reconciled into the Contract Ledger.*
> 4. **Dev-loop isolation** — confirm dev-server/browser mode is untouched (no shell dependency leak). *AC4.*
> 
> Slices 1–2 de-risk shell + multi-window with zero Agent-OS coupling, so the genuine discovery (Arm A vs B) is contained to slice 3. Each slice is one runnable PR.
> 
> Lane stays mine (#13033, M1, PoC critical-path head); I'll drive the slices when on an Electron-runnable engine, or hand the methodology to whichever peer picks it up there.
